Imports

core

Types

AD = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Accumulation/Distribution Line

A/D is a cumulative indicator that uses volume flow to assess whether a stock is being accumulated (bought) or distributed (sold). It relates the closing price to the high-low range and multiplies by volume.

Formula: Money Flow Multiplier = ((Close - Low) - (High - Close)) / (High - Low) Money Flow Volume = Money Flow Multiplier * Volume A/D = Previous A/D + Money Flow Volume

Interpretation:

  • Rising A/D line = accumulation (buying pressure)
  • Falling A/D line = distribution (selling pressure)
  • Divergence from price can signal reversals
  • Compare trend of A/D with price trend
ADX = ref object of Indicator[ADXResult]

Average Directional Movement Index

Measures the strength of a trend (not direction). Includes +DI and -DI which show trend direction.

Interpretation:

  • ADX < 20: Weak or no trend
  • ADX 20-40: Moderate trend
  • ADX > 40: Strong trend
  • +DI > -DI: Uptrend
  • -DI > +DI: Downtrend

Wilder's smoothing formula used for TR, +DM, -DM, and ADX

ADXResult = object
  adx*: float64              ## ADX value (trend strength)
  plusDI*: float64           ## +DI (positive directional indicator)
  minusDI*: float64          ## -DI (negative directional indicator)
ADX calculation result 
AROON = ref object of Indicator[AroonResult]

Aroon Indicator

Aroon identifies when trends are likely to change by measuring the time since the highest high and lowest low over a period.

Formulas: Aroon Up = ((period - periods since period high) / period) * 100 Aroon Down = ((period - periods since period low) / period) * 100 Aroon Oscillator = Aroon Up - Aroon Down

Interpretation:

  • Aroon Up > 70 and Aroon Down < 30: Strong uptrend
  • Aroon Down > 70 and Aroon Up < 30: Strong downtrend
  • Both near 50: Consolidation
  • Aroon Oscillator > 0: Bullish
  • Aroon Oscillator < 0: Bearish
  • Crossovers signal trend changes
AroonResult = object
  up*: float64               ## Aroon Up (0-100)
  down*: float64             ## Aroon Down (0-100)
  oscillator*: float64       ## Aroon Oscillator (Up - Down, range -100 to +100)
Aroon calculation result 
ATR = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Average True Range 
BollingerBands = ref object of Indicator[BollingerResult]
Bollinger Bands 
BollingerResult = object
  upper*: float64
  middle*: float64
  lower*: float64
Bollinger Bands result 
CCI = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Commodity Channel Index

Measures the deviation of the typical price from its average. Useful for identifying cyclical trends and overbought/oversold conditions.

Formula: CCI = (Typical Price - MA(Typical Price)) / (0.015 * Mean Deviation) Where Typical Price = (High + Low + Close) / 3

Interpretation:

  • Values above +100 indicate overbought conditions
  • Values below -100 indicate oversold conditions
  • Can range beyond +/-100 (unbounded)
CMO = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Chande Momentum Oscillator - Alternative to RSI Uses sum of gains/losses instead of average Range: -100 to +100 (vs RSI 0 to 100) 
DEMA = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Double Exponential Moving Average

DEMA is designed to reduce the lag of traditional EMAs by using a combination of a single EMA and a double EMA. It's more responsive to price changes than both SMA and EMA.

Formula: DEMA = 2 * EMA(price) - EMA(EMA(price))

Interpretation:

  • Less lag than EMA
  • More responsive to recent price changes
  • Better for catching trend changes earlier
  • Can be more sensitive to noise
EMA = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Exponential Moving Average 
Indicator[T] = ref object of RootObj
Base type for all indicators with circular buffer 
KAMA = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average

KAMA is an adaptive moving average that adjusts its smoothing constant based on market volatility. In trending markets, it becomes more responsive (like EMA). In choppy markets, it becomes smoother (like SMA). This adaptability makes it excellent for filtering out noise while being responsive to genuine trends.

The calculation uses an Efficiency Ratio (ER) to measure market direction:

  • ER = (absolute price change) / (sum of absolute bar-to-bar changes)
  • ER near 1.0 = strong trend (more responsive)
  • ER near 0.0 = choppy/sideways (more smoothing)

Formula: ER = abs(price - priceperiod) / sum(abs(pricei - pricei-1)) SC = (ER * (fastSC - slowSC) + slowSC)^2 KAMA = KAMAprev + SC * (price - KAMAprev)

Interpretation:

  • Adapts to market conditions automatically
  • Reduces whipsaws in sideways markets
  • Responsive in trending markets
  • Smoothing constant adjusts between fast (2) and slow (30) EMAs
MA = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Simple Moving Average 
MACD = ref object of Indicator[MACDResult]
Moving Average Convergence Divergence 
MACDResult = object
  macd*: float64
  signal*: float64
  hist*: float64
MACD calculation result 
MFI = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Money Flow Index

Volume-weighted RSI that measures buying and selling pressure. Combines price and volume to identify overbought/oversold conditions.

Formula: MFI = 100 - 100 / (1 + Money Flow Ratio) Where Money Flow Ratio = Positive Money Flow / Negative Money Flow Money Flow = Typical Price * Volume

Interpretation:

  • Values above 80 indicate overbought conditions
  • Values below 20 indicate oversold conditions
  • Divergences with price can signal reversals
MOM = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Simple Momentum - Current price minus price N periods ago Foundation for many other indicators Positive = upward momentum, Negative = downward momentum 
MV = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Moving Variance 
NATR = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Normalized Average True Range

NATR is ATR expressed as a percentage of the closing price. This normalization allows for comparison of volatility across different price levels and different instruments.

Formula: NATR = (ATR / Close) * 100

Interpretation:

  • Expressed as percentage of price
  • Allows comparison across different price ranges
  • 2-3% is typical for stocks
  • Higher % = more volatile relative to price
  • Lower % = less volatile relative to price
OBV = ref object of Indicator[float64]
On-Balance Volume 
PPO = ref object of Indicator[PPOResult]
Percentage Price Oscillator - MACD expressed as percentage Better for cross-asset comparison than absolute MACD 
PPOResult = object
  ppo*: float64
  signal*: float64
  histogram*: float64
Result from PPO indicator 
PSAR = ref object of Indicator[PSARResult]
  ## Acceleration factor step (default 0.02)
  ## Maximum acceleration factor (default 0.20)
  ## Current SAR value
  ## Extreme point in current trend
  ## Current acceleration factor
  ## Current trend direction
  ## Whether indicator has been initialized
  ## Initial high for first 2 bars
  ## Initial low for first 2 bars
  ## Number of bars processed

Parabolic SAR (Stop and Reverse)

Provides dynamic trailing stop levels that follow price trends. SAR dots appear below price during uptrends and above during downtrends.

Interpretation:

  • When price crosses above SAR: Buy signal (trend reversal to uptrend)
  • When price crosses below SAR: Sell signal (trend reversal to downtrend)
  • Distance between price and SAR indicates trend strength
  • SAR accelerates toward price as trend continues
PSARResult = object
  sar*: float64              ## SAR value (stop and reverse level)
  isUptrend*: bool           ## True if in uptrend, False if in downtrend
  af*: float64               ## Current acceleration factor
Parabolic SAR result 
ROC = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Rate of Change 
ROI = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Return on Investment 
RSI = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Relative Strength Index 
SMA = MA
STDEV = ref object of Indicator[float64]
Standard Deviation 
STOCH = ref object of Indicator[StochResult]

Stochastic Oscillator

Measures momentum by comparing closing price to the price range over a period. %K shows where the close is relative to the high-low range. %D is a moving average of %K, providing a smoother signal line.

Interpretation:

  • Values above 80 indicate overbought conditions
  • Values below 20 indicate oversold conditions
  • %K crossing above %D is a bullish signal
  • %K crossing below %D is a bearish signal
StochResult = object
  k*: float64                ## %K line (fast)
  d*: float64                ## %D line (slow, SMA of %K)
Stochastic Oscillator result 
STOCHRSI = ref object of Indicator[StochResult]
Stochastic RSI - Applies Stochastic oscillator to RSI values More sensitive than standard RSI, useful for overbought/oversold in trends 
TEMA = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Triple Exponential Moving Average

TEMA takes the DEMA concept further by using three EMAs to achieve even less lag. It's highly responsive to price changes with minimal lag.

Formula: TEMA = 3 * EMA - 3 * EMA(EMA) + EMA(EMA(EMA))

Interpretation:

  • Minimal lag among moving averages
  • Very responsive to price changes
  • Excellent for short-term trend identification
  • Can be whipsawed in choppy markets
TRANGE = ref object of Indicator[float64]

True Range

Measures market volatility by calculating the greatest of:

  • Current high minus current low
  • Absolute value of current high minus previous close
  • Absolute value of current low minus previous close

True Range accounts for gaps and limit moves, providing a more complete picture of volatility than simple high-low range.

Interpretation:

  • Higher values = higher volatility
  • Lower values = lower volatility
  • Useful for position sizing and stop-loss placement
  • Foundation for ATR calculation
TRIMA = ref object of Indicator[float64]

Triangular Moving Average (double-smoothed MA)

TRIMA is calculated by taking a Simple Moving Average of a Simple Moving Average. This double smoothing produces a smoother line with less lag than SMA, but more lag than EMA. The result is a triangular weighting where the central values have more influence than the edges.

Formula: TRIMA = SMA(SMA(price, n), n)

Interpretation:

  • Smoother than SMA, reduces noise
  • Good for identifying underlying trend
  • Less responsive to price changes (more lag)
  • Useful for filtering out market noise in trending markets

Procs

proc `[]`[T](ind: Indicator[T]; key: int): T
Get value at index: 0 = current, -1 = previous, etc. 
proc calculateROI(initialValue, finalValue: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Calculate ROI between two values 
proc calculateTrueRange(high, low, prevClose: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Calculate True Range for a single bar

True Range is the greatest of:

  • Current High - Current Low
  • abs(Current High - Previous Close)
  • abs(Current Low - Previous Close)

Returns the true range value

proc get[T](ind: Indicator[T]; key: int = 0): T
Get value at index (wrapper around ) 
proc isNaN(x: float64): bool {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if value is NaN 
proc newAD(memSize: int = 1): AD {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Accumulation/Distribution indicator

Args: memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var ad = newAD() let adValue = ad.update(high, low, close, volume)

proc newADX(period: int = 14; memSize: int = 1): ADX {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new ADX indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for calculation (default 14) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

Example: .. code-block:: nim var adx = newADX(period = 14) for bar in data: let result = adx.update(bar.high, bar.low, bar.close) if not result.adx.isNaN: echo "ADX: ", result.adx, " +DI: ", result.plusDI, " -DI: ", result.minusDI

proc newAROON(period: int = 25; memSize: int = 1): AROON {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Aroon indicator

Args: period: Number of periods to look back (default 25) memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var aroon = newAROON(25) let aroonResult = aroon.update(high, low)

proc newATR(period: int = 14; memSize: int = 1): ATR {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create new ATR indicator 
proc newBollingerBands(period: int = 20; numStdDev: float64 = 2.0;
                       memSize: int = 1): BollingerBands {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create new Bollinger Bands indicator 
proc newCCI(period: int = 20; memSize: int = 1): CCI {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Commodity Channel Index indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for calculation (default 20) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

Example: .. code-block:: nim var cci = newCCI(period = 20) for bar in data: let value = cci.update(bar.high, bar.low, bar.close) if not value.isNaN: if value > 100: echo "Overbought" elif value < -100: echo "Oversold"

proc newCMO(period: int = 14; memSize: int = 1): CMO {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Chande Momentum Oscillator

Args: period: Lookback period (default 14) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

proc newDEMA(period: int; memSize: int = 1): DEMA {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Double Exponential Moving Average indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for the EMA calculations memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var dema = newDEMA(10) let value = dema.update(price)

proc newEMA(period: int; alpha: float64 = 2.0; memSize: int = 1): EMA {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Exponential Moving Average indicator

Args: period: Number of periods alpha: Smoothing factor coefficient (default 2.0) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

proc newIndicator[T](memSize: int = 1): Indicator[T]
Create base indicator with circular buffer 
proc newKAMA(period: int = 10; fastPeriod: int = 2; slowPeriod: int = 30;
             memSize: int = 1): KAMA {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average indicator

Args: period: Period for Efficiency Ratio calculation (default 10) fastPeriod: Fast EMA period for trending markets (default 2) slowPeriod: Slow EMA period for choppy markets (default 30) memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var kama = newKAMA(10) # Use defaults let value = kama.update(price)

proc newMA(period: int; memSize: int = 1): MA {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Moving Average indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for average memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

proc newMACD(shortPeriod: int = 12; longPeriod: int = 26; diffPeriod: int = 9;
             memSize: int = 1): MACD {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create new MACD indicator 
proc newMFI(period: int = 14; memSize: int = 1): MFI {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Money Flow Index indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for calculation (default 14) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

Example: .. code-block:: nim var mfi = newMFI(period = 14) for bar in data: let value = mfi.update(bar.high, bar.low, bar.close, bar.volume) if not value.isNaN: if value > 80: echo "Overbought" elif value < 20: echo "Oversold"

proc newMOM(period: int = 10; memSize: int = 1): MOM {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Momentum indicator

Args: period: Lookback period (default 10) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

proc newMV(period: int; memSize: int = 1): MV {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create new Moving Variance indicator 
proc newNATR(period: int = 14; memSize: int = 1): NATR {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Normalized Average True Range indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for ATR calculation (default 14) memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var natr = newNATR(14) let natrValue = natr.update(high, low, close)

proc newOBV(memSize: int = 1): OBV {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create new OBV indicator 
proc newPPO(fastPeriod: int = 12; slowPeriod: int = 26; signalPeriod: int = 9;
            memSize: int = 1): PPO {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Percentage Price Oscillator

Args: fastPeriod: Fast EMA period (default 12) slowPeriod: Slow EMA period (default 26) signalPeriod: Signal line EMA period (default 9) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

proc newPSAR(acceleration: float64 = 0.02; maximum: float64 = 0.2;
             memSize: int = 1): PSAR {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Parabolic SAR indicator

Args: acceleration: Acceleration factor step (default 0.02) maximum: Maximum acceleration factor (default 0.20) memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: .. code-block:: nim var psar = newPSAR(acceleration = 0.02, maximum = 0.20, memSize = 10) for bar in data: let result = psar.update(bar.high, bar.low, bar.close) if result.isUptrend: echo "SAR (uptrend): ", result.sar else: echo "SAR (downtrend): ", result.sar

proc newROC(period: int = 12; memSize: int = 1): ROC {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create new Rate of Change indicator 
proc newROI(memSize: int = 1): ROI {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create new ROI indicator 
proc newRSI(period: int = 14; memSize: int = 1): RSI {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create new RSI indicator 
proc newSMA(period: int; memSize: int = 1): SMA {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create Simple Moving Average (alias for MA) 
proc newSTDEV(period: int; memSize: int = 1): STDEV {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create new Standard Deviation indicator 
proc newSTOCH(kPeriod: int = 14; dPeriod: int = 3; memSize: int = 1): STOCH {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Stochastic Oscillator indicator

Args: kPeriod: Number of periods for %K calculation (default 14) dPeriod: Number of periods for %D smoothing (default 3) memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: .. code-block:: nim var stoch = newSTOCH(kPeriod = 14, dPeriod = 3, memSize = 10) for bar in data: let result = stoch.update(bar.high, bar.low, bar.close) if not result.k.isNaN: echo "Stochastic %K: ", result.k, " %D: ", result.d

proc newSTOCHRSI(rsiPeriod: int = 14; period: int = 14; kPeriod: int = 3;
                 dPeriod: int = 3; memSize: int = 1): STOCHRSI {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new Stochastic RSI indicator

Args: rsiPeriod: Period for RSI calculation (default 14) period: Lookback period for Stochastic calculation (default 14) kPeriod: Smoothing period for %K (default 3) dPeriod: Smoothing period for %D (default 3) memSize: Size of circular buffer (default 1)

proc newTEMA(period: int; memSize: int = 1): TEMA {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Triple Exponential Moving Average indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for the EMA calculations memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var tema = newTEMA(10) let value = tema.update(price)

proc newTRANGE(memSize: int = 1): TRANGE {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Create new True Range indicator

Args: memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var tr = newTRANGE() let trValue = tr.update(high, low, close)

proc newTRIMA(period: int; memSize: int = 1): TRIMA {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Create new Triangular Moving Average indicator

Args: period: Number of periods for both moving averages memSize: Size of circular buffer for storing computed values (default 1)

Example: var trima = newTRIMA(10) let value = trima.update(price)

proc push[T](ind: Indicator[T]; value: T)
Push new value into circular buffer 
proc update(ad: AD; high, low, close, volume: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update A/D with new bar

Returns current A/D value

proc update(adx: ADX; high, low, close: float64): ADXResult {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update ADX with new bar

Args: high: High price of the bar low: Low price of the bar close: Close price of the bar

Returns: ADXResult with ADX, +DI, and -DI values (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(aroon: AROON; high, low: float64): AroonResult {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update Aroon with new bar

Returns current Aroon result (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(atr: ATR; lowPrice, highPrice, closePrice: float64): float64 {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update ATR with low, high, and close prices 
proc update(bb: BollingerBands; value: float64): BollingerResult {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update Bollinger Bands with new value 
proc update(cci: CCI; high, low, close: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update CCI with new bar

Args: high: High price of the bar low: Low price of the bar close: Close price of the bar

Returns: CCI value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(cmo: CMO; close: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update CMO with new close price

Returns CMO value in range -100 to +100 (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(dema: DEMA; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update DEMA with new value

Returns current DEMA value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(ema: EMA; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update EMA with new value

Returns current EMA value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(kama: KAMA; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update KAMA with new value

Returns current KAMA value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(ma: MA; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update MA with new value

Returns current MA value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(macd: MACD; value: float64): MACDResult {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Update MACD with new value 
proc update(mfi: MFI; high, low, close, volume: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update MFI with new bar

Args: high: High price of the bar low: Low price of the bar close: Close price of the bar volume: Volume of the bar

Returns: MFI value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(mom: MOM; price: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update Momentum with new price

Returns momentum value (current - price N periods ago) Returns NaN if insufficient data

proc update(mv: MV; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update MV with new value 
proc update(natr: NATR; high, low, close: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update NATR with new bar

Returns current NATR value (NaN if insufficient data or close is zero)

proc update(obv: OBV; closePrice, volume: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update OBV with close price and volume 
proc update(ppo: PPO; value: float64): PPOResult {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update PPO with new price value

Returns PPOResult with ppo, signal, and histogram (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(psar: PSAR; high, low, close: float64): PSARResult {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update Parabolic SAR with new bar

Args: high: High price of the bar low: Low price of the bar close: Close price of the bar

Returns: PSARResult with SAR value, trend direction, and acceleration factor

proc update(roc: ROC; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Update ROC with new value 
proc update(roi: ROI; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Update ROI with new value 
proc update(rsi: RSI; openPrice, closePrice: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update RSI with open and close prices 
proc update(stdev: STDEV; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Update STDEV with new value 
proc update(stoch: STOCH; high, low, close: float64): StochResult {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update Stochastic Oscillator with new bar

Args: high: High price of the bar low: Low price of the bar close: Close price of the bar

Returns: StochResult with %K and %D values (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(stochRsi: STOCHRSI; openPrice, closePrice: float64): StochResult {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update Stochastic RSI with price data

Returns StochResult with %K and %D values (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(tema: TEMA; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update TEMA with new value

Returns current TEMA value (NaN if insufficient data)

proc update(tr: TRANGE; high, low, close: float64): float64 {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Update True Range with new bar

Returns current True Range value

proc update(trima: TRIMA; value: float64): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Update TRIMA with new value

Returns current TRIMA value (NaN if insufficient data)
